messages.content:
edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
The old girlfriend of Carol and her husband get detained
as an accessory, and later they are convicted by the law, as
the ones who worked for Bob against considerable money.
Bob had never imagined being arrested that quickly by
his pupil. He compromises with authorities and reveals his
major business partners’ names against getting
commutation for his sentence.
Through Bob’s cooperation, two ministers in Europe
and a US Federal Judge resign. Many temple owners and
their affiliated attorneys, police officers, doctors, surgeons,
nurses, hospital managers, and many more got arrested in
Asia, Europe, and South America.
Bob’s conviction reduced from lifetime prison to 50
years without parole in Federal Security Prison against of
his cooperation, and for commutation.

Carol's old girlfriend and her husband were detained as accessories, ultimately convicted for working alongside Bob in exchange for a hefty sum of money. The swiftness with which his former student apprehended him caught Bob completely off guard. In a bid to lighten his sentence, Bob yielded to the authorities' demands and provided them with the identities of his major business partners.

As a result of Bob's cooperation, two European ministers and a Federal Judge from the United States were forced to resign. The repercussions of his actions rippled across continents, leading to the arrest of numerous temple owners, their affiliated attorneys, police officers, doctors, surgeons, nurses, hospital managers, and countless others in Asia, Europe, and South America.

Bob's punishment was somewhat mitigated due to his collaboration. Instead of a lifelong incarceration, his sentence was reduced to fifty years in a Federal Security Prison, without the possibility of parole. The offer of commutation enticed him to divulge critical information.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
But the strangest news is that NASA and the European
Space Agency have announced that the two asteroids have
changed direction and are no longer a threat to Earth.
Many NGOs and the media are protesting in different
countries. Why didn’t governments inform their people
earlier about asteroids hitting Earth?
Well, the authorities had clear answers to these
questions: “Informing people about the catastrophe could
not change anything, except creating chaos, more madness,
looting, and sabotage.”

However, the most bewildering revelation came from NASA and the European Space Agency, who astonished the world by proclaiming that the once menacing asteroids had inexplicably altered their trajectory, sparing Earth from their potential devastation. This revelation sparked widespread discontent among non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and the media, igniting protests in countries far and wide. People demanded to know why their respective governments had not forewarned them of this looming threat.

Attempting to address the mounting concerns, the authorities finally offered their explanation: "Informing the public about the imminent catastrophe would have only resulted in futile panic, chaos, and lawlessness. The potential aftermath would have been plagued by looting, rampant madness, and widespread sabotage."

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
Breaking News: “No trace of the older man who
claimed to be the prophet Noah and his under construction
ship. He and his ship are no longer in Persia, and no one
knows when he and his ship vanished.”
And even his sons have no idea of the conditions of his
disappearance.
Of course, this was a good event for media and the
market of rumours, jokes, and many speculations.
Some believe him, and some say it was a plan by NASA
and other space agencies to inform the public directly or
indirectly about the threat.
But the truth was what Gabriel said, “Annihilation of
Earth was certain, which was changed by God’s will. And
the older man was indeed the Prophet Noah.”

Breaking News flashes across screens: "The enigmatic figure who had boldly claimed to be the lost prophet Noah has vanished, leaving no trace behind. His partially constructed ship, too, has mysteriously disappeared from the heart of Persia, shrouded in an inexplicable veil of secrecy. Even the prophet's own sons remain perplexed, ignorant of the circumstances surrounding his sudden and unexplained disappearance.

As news of this extraordinary occurrence spread like wildfire, the media and gossip mills burst into a frenzy. Rumors, jokes, and countless speculations flourished, captivating the collective imagination of both believers and skeptics alike. Some staunchly believed in the authenticity of the prophet and his celestial mission, while others cynically dismissed it as an elaborate ruse orchestrated by NASA or other space agencies, intended to alert or mislead the public concerning an impending cosmic threat.

Yet, beneath the web of conjecture, the truth stood resolute, resonating with the words once whispered by Gabriel himself: "The annihilation of Earth was an irrefutable certainty, altered solely by the divine intervention of God's will. And that older man, beyond all doubt, was indeed the Prophet Noah."

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
The Queen of Persia grants I, Ali, Colonel Iranmardy,
and Colonel David and some American and Persian special
police forces, the Badge of Honor. And also, Her Majesty
grants cash prizes for our active security participants,
ranging from 100,000 to 500,000 dollars.
Carol is still in shock, and she cannot believe that we
have survived from a danger that could have perished us all,
although we are now celebrities and media icons. Notably,
the American news agencies one after another are the ones
who are the most eager for having us interviewed.
Now, many Paparazzi and reporters gather around our
house every day and asking us for an interview or a photo.
We try to stay away from them as much as possible to avoid
confronting them, but we have barely managed to escape
the media. So, Carol suggests, “We can travel to some
European countries for a few months to get a rest and away
from the crowd.”

The Queen of Persia, in recognition of our unwavering dedication, bestows upon me, Ali, Colonel Iranmardy, Colonel David, and a select group of American and Persian special police forces the distinguished Badge of Honor. Additionally, Her Majesty generously awards generous cash prizes to our valiant security participants, ranging from $100,000 to $500,000.

Carol, still reeling from the shock, finds it difficult to fathom that we have emerged from the clutches of a peril that had the potential to obliterate us all. Astonishingly, we find ourselves transformed into celebrated figures and media sensations. Amongst the clamor, it is the American news agencies that eagerly vie to secure interviews, each one striving to be the first in line to unravel our incredible tale.

Our everyday existence is now besieged by hoards of paparazzi and persistent reporters, their incessant demands for interviews and snapshots attesting to their unrelenting pursuit. An earnest effort is made to keep our distance, steering clear of confrontations, but we have only managed to elude the media circus by the narrowest of margins. Thus, Carol suggests a respite from the tumultuous atmosphere, proposing that we seek solace in the tranquil embrace of various European countries for a few precious months.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
We consult with our parents. They also encourage us to
go to Europe, but Grandpa suggests we take a tour around
Persia and visit the ancient cities, landmarks, and museums
in Persia. I call Colonel David and asking him for a leave of
a few months. Colonel David says, “It could be a good idea,
but I want you to come to the embassy tomorrow, so we can
discuss it.”
When I arrive at the embassy, one of my colleagues asks
me to go to the secured room where Colonel David and the
Iranmardy are waiting for me.
I’m shocked and think our mission has not been over
yet.
When I enter the safe room and close the door, what I
see is more shocking than ever, the ambassador, Colonel
David, Colonel Iranmardy, and Ali standing politely
straight. And the person sitting in the chair with his back to
the door, looks familiar.

Seeking guidance, we turn to our parents for advice. They too advocate for a European escapade, yet Grandpa, with his penchant for nostalgia, suggests an alternative: embarking on a journey across Persia, where we would be immersed in the rich tapestry of ancient cities, renowned landmarks, and captivating museums. Intrigued by his proposition, I promptly reach out to Colonel David to request a leave of absence for a few months. To my surprise, he proposes a meeting at the embassy the following day to discuss the matter further.

As I arrive at the embassy, a colleague ushers me towards a secured room, where Colonel David and Colonel Iranmardy await my arrival. A sense of apprehension washes over me; it appears that our mission is far from reaching its conclusion.

Upon entering the sanctity of the safe room, what unfolds before my eyes leaves me utterly astounded. The ambassador, Colonel David, Colonel Iranmardy, and Ali all stand erect and composed. And there, seated with his back turned towards the door, a wave of familiarity washes over me, bringing with it a startling realization.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
When the person turns the chair, I am surprised to see
Gabriel.
“Well, well, well, John, the Knight of the Fourth
World,” says Gabriel.
I’m surprised by what I see and cannot react for a few
seconds. And then I look at the people behind Gabriel, and
I ask, “Have you all played a part in this plan from the
beginning?”
I feel they have insulted to my intelligence, and I say
my last word unofficially. Although what I said unofficially
and to some extent impolitely, makes everyone who is
standing behind Gabriel burst to laugh.
Gabriel goes on without paying attention to what I said,
and says “Traveling and taking sightseeing, touring around
Iran, and visiting the ancient monuments and landmarks is
good for you and your family, and I will be along with you
in this journey.”
“According to the Secretary of State, all your expenses
are covered by the government, and your trip with your
family is planned and starts tomorrow,” The ambassador
adds, “I’m confused and do not know if this is an idea from
Gabriel or my grandfather.”
The only thing that is clear to me is that they all are the
followers of Gabriel. Everybody in the secured room is
standing respectfully and politely at the presence of Gabriel,
but I look at Gabriel as an old friend or even annoying friend
again, although I don’t wish to expose my private
relationship with him to others.

As the chair pivots, revealing the face of the occupant, my astonishment knows no bounds. It is Gabriel.

"Well, well, well, John, the Knight of the Fourth World," Gabriel utters nonchalantly.

I find myself momentarily dumbfounded, struggling to gather my wits. After a few seconds, my gaze shifts towards the individuals standing behind Gabriel, and a question escapes my lips, laden with both surprise and suspicion. "Have you all been part of this plan from the very beginning?"

I cannot help but feel that they have underestimated my intelligence, and thus my retort becomes laced with a touch of informality. Unbeknownst to me, my impromptu words set off a chorus of laughter, the assembled group finding amusement in my seemingly brusque reaction.

Gabriel, undeterred by my response, continues without acknowledging it. "Embarking on a journey through Iran, exploring its sights, ancient monuments, and landmarks will be beneficial for you and your family," he asserts. "And rest assured, I shall accompany you on this voyage."

"As per the Secretary of State, all your expenses will be covered by the government, and your trip has been meticulously planned, commencing tomorrow," adds the ambassador. "To be honest, I am uncertain whether this idea originated from Gabriel or your grandfather."

In that moment, it becomes apparent to me that every individual in the secure room is a devout follower of Gabriel. While they stand with unwavering respect and courtesy in his presence, I cannot help but regard Gabriel as an old friend, or at times, as an irksome companion. Regardless, I am disinclined to unveil the intricacies of our private relationship to others.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
Now I ask Gabriel for his permission, that I leave the
meeting and talk to Carrol about the idea, unaware that
Gabriel has already got Carol’s consent. “I’m going home
with a lot of amazing news.”
Although Carol had previously agreed to take a tour
around Persia, she did not know about those attending the
embassy meeting.
When I tell her about the meeting and the people present
in there, she stares at me in surprise for a minute.

Seeking Gabriel's permission to excuse myself from the meeting, I express my desire to discuss the idea with Carol, oblivious to the fact that Gabriel had already obtained her consent. "I'll be heading home with a treasure trove of astonishing news," I proclaim.

Unbeknownst to Carol, although she had agreed to embark on a tour of Persia, she remained unaware of the individuals who would be present at the embassy meeting.

As I relay the events of the meeting and disclose the identities of those in attendance, a profound sense of surprise envelopes Carol, leaving her momentarily speechless.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
As planned, the embassy’s travel department is arranging
an exciting trip around Persia for me and my family.
They have hired an experienced nurse to take care of
our child during the journey and provided us with a private
single carriage so that we can travel by train comfortably.
Our first stop is at the ancient city of Yazd. Our wagon
is detached from the main engine, so we could stay for a few
days and visit the historical sights.
We stay in a four-star hotel in Yazd and then go to
Kashan and Maimand and observe the 7,000 years of human
civilization.
After a few days staying in Yazd province, our wagon
connects to the next train and now, we are headed to
Isfahan.
We visit the incredible 600-year-old architecture of
Isfahan, where the monuments still stand firmly on their feet
and say, “We are the beauty of Persian architecture.”
One of Isfahan’s most notable landmarks is a public
bath built 600 years ago using methane gas from its sewage
as a fuel for making hot water.

As per the meticulous plans of the embassy's travel department, an enchanting journey across Persia awaits me and my family. To ensure our comfort and convenience, they have conscientiously hired an experienced nurse to attend to our child's needs during the trip. Additionally, a private and spacious carriage has been allocated to us, enabling us to traverse the country by train in utmost comfort.

Our inaugural destination is the ancient city of Yazd. The carriage is detached from the main engine, allowing us to reside in this historical enclave for a few days, exploring its breathtaking sights. We find repose in a delightful four-star hotel nestled within Yazd, before proceeding to Kashan and Maimand, where the impressive tapestry of human civilization unfurls before our very eyes, spanning an astonishing 7,000 years.

After a few days' sojourn in Yazd province, our carriage is patiently reconnected to the next train, and we embark on our journey towards Isfahan, a city steeped in a 600-year-old magnificence. As we venture deeper into Isfahan, we bear witness to its awe-inspiring architectural wonders, monuments that have withstood the test of time, proudly proclaiming, "We are the epitome of Persian splendor."

Among the many notable landmarks in Isfahan lies a public bath, a testament to the ingenuity of its creators. Built six centuries ago, this architectural marvel ingeniously harnessed the power of methane gas from its sewage system to fuel the production of hot water, a remarkable feat indeed.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
Carol asks me, “How could they know of the technique
conserving energy and using environment-friendly fuel 600
years ago?”
“Look at the writings on the monument that states that
the founder of the method, has been Sheikh Bahaie, the great mystic,
philosopher and scientist,” I answer.
I continue modestly, “I’m not sure, but I know they are
one of the ancient civilizations and have done considerable
research in the basic sciences that are the basis of modern
science and technology of today.”

Carol poses a question that lingers in the air, "How could they have possessed knowledge of energy conservation and utilized environmentally friendly fuel six centuries ago?"

I pause for a moment, allowing the grandeur of our surroundings to engulf us. "Take a careful look at the inscriptions on this architectural marvel," I respond. "They attribute the creation of this technique to none other than Sheikh Bahaie, a revered mystic, philosopher, and scientist."

Modesty tinges my voice as I continue, "While I cannot claim absolute certainty, it is evident that this great ancient civilization delved deeply into the realm of basic sciences, laying the foundation for the modern scientific advancements and technological marvels we witness today."

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel:
Although what we have seen so far was worth seeing,
Carol and I are eager to visit Shiraz and Persepolis, about
which we have heard many things before.
Finally, the train reaches the city of Shiraz, our wagon
gets detached from the train, and when we get off, a man
from the traveling department in a Black Ford Continental
of the Consulate is waiting for us at the railway station.
We are taken to a luxurious hotel near Persepolis to stay.
The next day, after having our breakfast, we go to visit
the glorious monuments of Persepolis that is probably one
of the precious heritages of human civilization.

Our thirst for exploration remains unquenched as Carol and I eagerly anticipate our visit to Shiraz and Persepolis, historical sites that have long captured our imaginations.

Finally, our train reaches the captivating city of Shiraz, and as our carriage is detached from the locomotive, we disembark to find a courteous representative from the consulate's travel department awaiting us in a sleek Black Ford Continental. With utmost care and efficiency, he whisks us away to a luxurious hotel situated in close proximity to Persepolis, ensuring our stay is nothing short of opulence.

The following day, fortified by a delightful breakfast, we embark on a grand adventure to behold the resplendent monuments that radiate through the ancient ruins of Persepolis. This archaeological treasure stands as a testament to the magnificence
